Module me.stringdotjar.flixelgdx.core
Package me.stringdotjar.flixelgdx.audio

Class FlixelSound

java.lang.Object
me.stringdotjar.flixelgdx.FlixelBasic
me.stringdotjar.flixelgdx.audio.FlixelSound
All Implemented Interfaces:
com.badlogic.gdx.utils.Disposable, com.badlogic.gdx.utils.Pool.Poolable, FlixelAsset<games.rednblack.miniaudio.MASound>, FlixelDestroyable, FlixelDrawable, FlixelUpdatable
public class FlixelSound extends FlixelBasic implements FlixelAsset<games.rednblack.miniaudio.MASound>
Flixel sound object that wraps an MASound from the miniaudio library.

Provides volume, pitch, pan, play/pause/stop/resume, fade-in/fade-out, position (time), optional miniaudio graph effects (addReverb(float), addEcho(float, float), addLowPassMuffle(double), attachCustomNode(games.rednblack.miniaudio.MANode)), and an onComplete signal when the sound finishes (for non-looping sounds).

This class implements FlixelAsset<MASound> for a shared refcount / persist contract. persist controls whether this FlixelSound is treated as long-lived in game state (e.g. not killed on substate switches). It is separate from FlixelAssetManager.clearNonPersist(), which clears pooled typed handles and wrappers on the global asset manager—not miniaudio instances created directly from a FileHandle. Use retain() / release() if you mirror pooled-asset semantics for sounds you manage manually.

See Also:
  • FlixelAssetManager.resolveAudioPath(String)

    • getMASound

    • queueLoad

      public void queueLoad()
      Description copied from interface: FlixelAsset
      Enqueues this asset into the active FlixelAssetManager. Safe to call multiple times.

      Use this method to preload assets in your game's loading state.

      Specified by:
      queueLoad in interface FlixelAsset<games.rednblack.miniaudio.MASound>

    • getTime

      public float getTime()
      Current playback position in milliseconds.

      If set while paused, the change takes effect after resume().

      to - Target volume (typically 0).

      this, for chaining.

    • update

      public void update(float elapsed)
      Description copied from class: FlixelBasic
      Updates the logic of this FlixelBasic.

      Override this function to update your object's position and appearance. This is where most game rules and behavioral code will go.

      Specified by:
      update in interface FlixelUpdatable
      Overrides:
      update in class FlixelBasic
      Parameters:
      elapsed - Seconds elapsed since the last frame.

    • clearAudioEffectChain

      public void clearAudioEffectChain()
      Detaches and disposes every node in audioEffectNodes (reverse order). Called from FlixelBasic.reset() and destroy().

    • addReverb

      @NotNull public @NotNull FlixelSound addReverb(float wetAmount)
      Appends a reverb node with the given wet amount in [0, 1] (dry is set to 1 - wet). Build effect chains in load/setup code, not every frame.

    • addEcho

      @NotNull public @NotNull FlixelSound addEcho(float delaySeconds, float decay)
      Appends a stereo delay/echo node (miniaudio MADelayNode).
      Parameters:
      delaySeconds - Delay time in seconds.
      decay - Decay factor for the delayed signal.

    • addLowPassMuffle

      @NotNull public @NotNull FlixelSound addLowPassMuffle(double cutoffHz)
      Appends a 2nd-order low-pass filter (muffled / distant sound).
      Parameters:
      cutoffHz - Cutoff frequency in Hz.

    • attachCustomNode

      @NotNull public @NotNull FlixelSound attachCustomNode(@NotNull @NotNull games.rednblack.miniaudio.MANode node)
      Expert escape hatch: append any MANode to the effect chain. this sound disposes the node when clearAudioEffectChain() runs unless you remove it yourself first.

    • destroy

      public void destroy()
      Description copied from class: FlixelBasic
      Cleans up this object so it can be garbage-collected. A destroyed FlixelBasic should not be used anymore. Use FlixelBasic.kill() if you only want to disable it temporarily and FlixelBasic.revive() it later.

      Override this function to clean up any resources used by this object, such as textures, fonts, sounds, etc.

      This function is called automatically when FlixelBasic.dispose() or FlixelBasic.reset() is executed, so you don't need to call it manually.

      Specified by:
      destroy in interface FlixelDestroyable
      Overrides:
      destroy in class FlixelBasic
      See Also:
      • FlixelBasic.dispose()
      • FlixelBasic.reset()
